source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Recognise and use fractions as numbers: unit fractions and non-unit fractions with small denominators.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack introduces tenths as equal parts of one whole and positions tenths on a number line.

objective.child_goal: I can split one whole into ten equal parts and name the tenths.
objective.statement: Recognise, build and place tenths as fractions of one whole.
misconceptions: Counts pieces without checking that the parts are equal.
Thinks the denominator is the number of shaded parts.
Places one tenth at 1 on the number line instead of between 0 and 1.

vocabulary.child_explanation: One of ten equal parts of a whole.
vocabulary.audio_script: A tenth is one equal part when the whole is split into ten.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: One highlighted tile out of ten equal tiles.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The bottom number that tells how many equal parts the whole has.
vocabulary.audio_script: The denominator tells how many equal parts make the whole.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: The number 10 under a fraction wall.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which shape is split into equal parts?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check equal-part understanding before naming tenths.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two explorer tiles compare equal and unequal cuts.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The island bridge has ten equal planks. Each plank is one tenth.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce tenths as one whole split into ten equal parts.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Ten equal planks make one whole bridge. One plank is one tenth.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A bridge appears with ten equal planks across a river.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Three shaded planks out of ten equal planks is three tenths.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model numerator and denominator meaning with a concrete representation.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The ten tells us the whole has ten equal parts. The three tells us three are shaded.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three planks glow while the denominator label stays under all ten.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Shade four of the ten equal parts.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Let the learner construct tenths before symbolic selection.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Count the equal parts you shade, but keep all ten parts in the whole.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A fraction wall with ten tappable equal tiles.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Where does one tenth go between 0 and 1?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect whether the learner treats one tenth as less than one whole.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: One tenth is one step out of ten between zero and one.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A number line from 0 to 1 split into ten equal jumps.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Rafi why three tenths is not the same as three wholes.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Can you show me what the ten means?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion tries to make three wholes and waits for correction.

manipulatives.purpose: Represent tenths as ten equal parts of one whole before number-line placement.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners shade, unshade and compare tenths on a wall and then map them to a number line.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard and tap controls select tiles; reduced-motion mode uses outlines instead of animated fills.

manipulatives.purpose: Connect a count of tenths to its exact position between zero and one without relying on shaded area.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners steer a numbered raft to tenths landing posts, use equal jumps to check the position and compare two rafts by which is farther along the river.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard arrow keys or direct number entry move the raft by one tenth; single-switch scanning cycles through labelled landing posts and confirm; static mode uses a numbered line with a movable text marker; reduced-motion mode changes position instantly and announces the fraction and its distance from zero.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave halves, quarters and tenths only after tenths as equal parts is stable.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build tenths with ten equal parts and distinguish equal from unequal partitions.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: whole shape + ten partition tiles + shade target
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check that visual partitions are genuinely equal and clear on small screens.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Probe whether learners understand top and bottom fraction numbers.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: fraction wall + symbolic choices + close numerator/denominator distractors
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Distractors must map to named misconceptions.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Place tenths between 0 and 1 and connect wall parts to line positions.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: 0-1 number line + ten ticks + target tenth
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include one tenth, five tenths and nine tenths before mixed placements.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Provide spaced review across walls, symbols and simple number-line positions.
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid decimals in this pack unless an extension route is selected.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Apply tenths to measure strips and simple explorer-map contexts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: context strip + ten equal units + shaded or travelled amount
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep contexts concrete and avoid ratio language.

body.prompt: Shade three tenths of the bridge.

interaction_route.touch: Tap equal parts, a labelled tenth position or a numbered choice; precise shading or marker dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the whole/model or number line; use arrows, numbered controls or direct fraction entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, whole/equal parts or line positions, choices/controls, check and retry in a fixed order.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: The whole bridge has ten equal planks.
Shade three of the ten planks.
question_variants.explanation: Three tenths means 3 out of 10 equal parts are shaded.

feedback.correct: “3/10” uses the numerator for selected parts and denominator 10 for all ten equal parts of the whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: The model uses one whole split into 10 equal parts with 3 selected, supporting 3/10.
feedback.repair: Count all equal parts in the whole for the denominator, then count only the selected or shaded parts for the numerator. Keep the denominator 10 even when the shaded count changes.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal parts make the whole, and how many of those parts are selected? Which number belongs below and above the fraction bar?
feedback.strategy_support: Use ALL EQUAL PARTS = DENOMINATOR → SELECTED PARTS = NUMERATOR → READ FRACTION.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

hints: The line from 0 to 1 has ten equal jumps.
One tenth is the first jump after 0.
question_variants.explanation: One tenth is one of ten equal steps between 0 and 1.

feedback.correct: “0.1” places or changes the fraction by equal one-tenth steps between 0 and 1 rather than treating one tenth as one whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: From 0 to 1 there are 10 equal jumps; 1/10 is 1 one-tenth jump(s) from 0, at 0.1.
feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

hints: The shaded number goes on top.
The total equal parts go on the bottom.
question_variants.explanation: Four out of ten equal parts is written as 4/10.

feedback.correct: “4/10” uses the numerator for selected parts and denominator 10 for all ten equal parts of the whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: Four out of ten equal parts is written as 4/10. The same whole and equal-part count support 4/10.
feedback.repair: Count all equal parts in the whole for the denominator, then count only the selected or shaded parts for the numerator. Keep the denominator 10 even when the shaded count changes.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal parts make the whole, and how many of those parts are selected? Which number belongs below and above the fraction bar?

hints: Check that all ten planks are equal in size.
Select 1 of the 10 equal planks.
question_variants.explanation: One tenths means 1 out of 10 equal parts.

feedback.correct: “1/10” keeps one whole split or regrouped into equal parts, so the fraction amount and any equivalence are valid.
feedback.representation_evidence: The model uses one whole split into 10 equal parts with 1 selected, supporting 1/10.
feedback.repair: Identify one agreed whole, check that all parts are equal, and only then count or regroup them. If part sizes differ, repartition the whole before naming a fraction or claiming equivalence.
feedback.check_prompt: Is there one agreed whole, and are every one of its parts equal before you count or regroup them?
feedback.strategy_support: Use WHOLE → CHECK EQUAL PARTS → COUNT/REGROUP → VERIFY SAME AMOUNT.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: Check that all ten sections are equal in size.
Select 7 of the 10 equal sections.
question_variants.explanation: Seven tenths means 7 out of 10 equal parts.

feedback.correct: “7/10” keeps one whole split or regrouped into equal parts, so the fraction amount and any equivalence are valid.
feedback.representation_evidence: The model uses one whole split into 10 equal parts with 7 selected, supporting 7/10.
feedback.repair: Identify one agreed whole, check that all parts are equal, and only then count or regroup them. If part sizes differ, repartition the whole before naming a fraction or claiming equivalence.
feedback.check_prompt: Is there one agreed whole, and are every one of its parts equal before you count or regroup them?
feedback.strategy_support: Use WHOLE → CHECK EQUAL PARTS → COUNT/REGROUP → VERIFY SAME AMOUNT.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: Check that all ten equal bands are equal in size.
Select 8 of the 10 equal equal bands.
question_variants.explanation: Eight tenths means 8 out of 10 equal parts.

feedback.correct: “8/10” keeps one whole split or regrouped into equal parts, so the fraction amount and any equivalence are valid.
feedback.representation_evidence: The model uses one whole split into 10 equal parts with 8 selected, supporting 8/10.
feedback.repair: Identify one agreed whole, check that all parts are equal, and only then count or regroup them. If part sizes differ, repartition the whole before naming a fraction or claiming equivalence.
feedback.check_prompt: Is there one agreed whole, and are every one of its parts equal before you count or regroup them?
feedback.strategy_support: Use WHOLE → CHECK EQUAL PARTS → COUNT/REGROUP → VERIFY SAME AMOUNT.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

hints: The numerator counts the marked parts.
The denominator counts all the equal parts in the whole.
question_variants.explanation: 1 marked parts out of 10 equal parts is 1/10. The numerator is 1 and the denominator is 10.

feedback.correct: “1/10” uses the numerator for selected parts and denominator 10 for all ten equal parts of the whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: The representation has 1 selected parts out of 10 equal parts, so numerator = 1 and denominator = 10; answer 1/10.
feedback.repair: Count all equal parts in the whole for the denominator, then count only the selected or shaded parts for the numerator. Keep the denominator 10 even when the shaded count changes.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal parts make the whole, and how many of those parts are selected? Which number belongs below and above the fraction bar?
feedback.strategy_support: Use ALL EQUAL PARTS = DENOMINATOR → SELECTED PARTS = NUMERATOR → READ FRACTION.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.

question_variants.explanation: 8 marked parts out of 10 equal parts is 8/10. The numerator is 8 and the denominator is 10.

feedback.correct: “8/10” uses the numerator for selected parts and denominator 10 for all ten equal parts of the whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: The representation has 8 selected parts out of 10 equal parts, so numerator = 8 and denominator = 10; answer 8/10.
feedback.repair: Count all equal parts in the whole for the denominator, then count only the selected or shaded parts for the numerator. Keep the denominator 10 even when the shaded count changes.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal parts make the whole, and how many of those parts are selected? Which number belongs below and above the fraction bar?
feedback.strategy_support: Use ALL EQUAL PARTS = DENOMINATOR → SELECTED PARTS = NUMERATOR → READ FRACTION.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

body.prompt: Nine of ten equal bridge planks are marked. Which fraction is marked?
body.keyboard_instructions: Use arrow keys to review choices, then Enter to select.

interaction_route.touch: Tap equal parts, a labelled tenth position or a numbered choice; precise shading or marker dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the whole/model or number line; use arrows, numbered controls or direct fraction entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, whole/equal parts or line positions, choices/controls, check and retry in a fixed order.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

body.prompt: Nine of ten equal map sections are marked. Which fraction is marked?
body.keyboard_instructions: Use arrow keys to review choices, then Enter to select.

interaction_route.touch: Tap equal parts, a labelled tenth position or a numbered choice; precise shading or marker dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the whole/model or number line; use arrows, numbered controls or direct fraction entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, whole/equal parts or line positions, choices/controls, check and retry in a fixed order.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, whole/equal parts or line positions, choices/controls, check and retry in a fixed order.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: The numerator counts the marked parts.
The denominator counts all the equal parts in the whole.
question_variants.explanation: 9 marked parts out of 10 equal parts is 9/10. The numerator is 9 and the denominator is 10.

feedback.correct: “9/10” uses the numerator for selected parts and denominator 10 for all ten equal parts of the whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: The representation has 9 selected parts out of 10 equal parts, so numerator = 9 and denominator = 10; answer 9/10.
feedback.repair: Count all equal parts in the whole for the denominator, then count only the selected or shaded parts for the numerator. Keep the denominator 10 even when the shaded count changes.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal parts make the whole, and how many of those parts are selected? Which number belongs below and above the fraction bar?
feedback.strategy_support: Use ALL EQUAL PARTS = DENOMINATOR → SELECTED PARTS = NUMERATOR → READ FRACTION.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: The numerator counts the marked parts.
The denominator counts all the equal parts in the whole.
question_variants.explanation: 9 marked parts out of 10 equal parts is 9/10. The numerator is 9 and the denominator is 10.

feedback.correct: “9/10” uses the numerator for selected parts and denominator 10 for all ten equal parts of the whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: The representation has 9 selected parts out of 10 equal parts, so numerator = 9 and denominator = 10; answer 9/10.
feedback.repair: Count all equal parts in the whole for the denominator, then count only the selected or shaded parts for the numerator. Keep the denominator 10 even when the shaded count changes.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal parts make the whole, and how many of those parts are selected? Which number belongs below and above the fraction bar?
feedback.strategy_support: Use ALL EQUAL PARTS = DENOMINATOR → SELECTED PARTS = NUMERATOR → READ FRACTION.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

body.prompt: Ten of ten equal gauge bands are marked. Which fraction is marked?
body.keyboard_instructions: Use arrow keys to review choices, then Enter to select.

interaction_route.touch: Tap equal parts, a labelled tenth position or a numbered choice; precise shading or marker dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the whole/model or number line; use arrows, numbered controls or direct fraction entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, whole/equal parts or line positions, choices/controls, check and retry in a fixed order.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

hints: The interval from 0 to 1 is split into ten equal jumps.
1/10 is the first tick after 0.
question_variants.explanation: 1/10 is 1 equal jumps of one tenth from 0.

feedback.correct: “0.1” places or changes the fraction by equal one-tenth steps between 0 and 1 rather than treating one tenth as one whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: From 0 to 1 there are 10 equal jumps; 1/10 is 1 one-tenth jump(s) from 0, at 0.1.
feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: The interval from 0 to 1 is split into ten equal jumps.
9/10 is the ninth tick after 0.
question_variants.explanation: 9/10 is 9 equal jumps of one tenth from 0.

feedback.correct: “0.9” places or changes the fraction by equal one-tenth steps between 0 and 1 rather than treating one tenth as one whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: From 0 to 1 there are 10 equal jumps; 9/10 is 9 one-tenth jump(s) from 0, at 0.9.
feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: The interval from 0 to 1 is split into ten equal jumps.
9/10 is the ninth tick after 0.
question_variants.explanation: 9/10 is 9 equal jumps of one tenth from 0.

feedback.correct: “0.9” places or changes the fraction by equal one-tenth steps between 0 and 1 rather than treating one tenth as one whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: From 0 to 1 there are 10 equal jumps; 9/10 is 9 one-tenth jump(s) from 0, at 0.9.
feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: The interval from 0 to 1 is split into ten equal jumps.
9/10 is the ninth tick after 0.
question_variants.explanation: 9/10 is 9 equal jumps of one tenth from 0.

feedback.correct: “0.9” places or changes the fraction by equal one-tenth steps between 0 and 1 rather than treating one tenth as one whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: From 0 to 1 there are 10 equal jumps; 9/10 is 9 one-tenth jump(s) from 0, at 0.9.
feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the whole/model or number line; use arrows, numbered controls or direct fraction entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, whole/equal parts or line positions, choices/controls, check and retry in a fixed order.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: The interval from 0 to 1 is split into ten equal jumps.
9/10 is the ninth tick after 0.
question_variants.explanation: 9/10 is 9 equal jumps of one tenth from 0.

feedback.correct: “0.9” places or changes the fraction by equal one-tenth steps between 0 and 1 rather than treating one tenth as one whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: From 0 to 1 there are 10 equal jumps; 9/10 is 9 one-tenth jump(s) from 0, at 0.9.
feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

hints: Count highlighted sections for the top number.
Count all equal sections for the bottom number.
question_variants.explanation: 1 of 10 equal route sections are highlighted, so the fraction is 1/10.

feedback.correct: “1/10” uses the numerator for selected parts and denominator 10 for all ten equal parts of the whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: The representation has 1 selected parts out of 10 equal parts, so numerator = 1 and denominator = 10; answer 1/10.
feedback.repair: Count all equal parts in the whole for the denominator, then count only the selected or shaded parts for the numerator. Keep the denominator 10 even when the shaded count changes.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal parts make the whole, and how many of those parts are selected? Which number belongs below and above the fraction bar?
feedback.strategy_support: Use ALL EQUAL PARTS = DENOMINATOR → SELECTED PARTS = NUMERATOR → READ FRACTION.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: Regroup the same ten equal parts without changing the amount.
Equivalent fractions cover the same amount of the same whole.
question_variants.explanation: One fifth covers two of ten equal parts, so 1/5 = 2/10.

feedback.correct: “2/10” keeps one whole split or regrouped into equal parts, so the fraction amount and any equivalence are valid.
feedback.representation_evidence: One fifth covers two of ten equal parts, so 1/5 = 2/10. The same whole and equal-part count support 2/10.
feedback.repair: Identify one agreed whole, check that all parts are equal, and only then count or regroup them. If part sizes differ, repartition the whole before naming a fraction or claiming equivalence.
feedback.check_prompt: Is there one agreed whole, and are every one of its parts equal before you count or regroup them?
feedback.strategy_support: Use WHOLE → CHECK EQUAL PARTS → COUNT/REGROUP → VERIFY SAME AMOUNT.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.

feedback.repair: Identify one agreed whole, check that all parts are equal, and only then count or regroup them. If part sizes differ, repartition the whole before naming a fraction or claiming equivalence.
feedback.check_prompt: Is there one agreed whole, and are every one of its parts equal before you count or regroup them?
feedback.strategy_support: Use WHOLE → CHECK EQUAL PARTS → COUNT/REGROUP → VERIFY SAME AMOUNT.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

body.prompt: Which model can show 10/10 correctly?
choices: Ten separate wholes, with 10 whole shapes shaded
One whole split into 10 equal parts, with 9 shaded
One whole split into 10 equal parts, with 10 shaded
One whole split into 10 unequal pieces, with 10 shaded
body.keyboard_instructions: Use arrow keys to review choices, then Enter to select.

interaction_route.touch: Tap equal parts, a labelled tenth position or a numbered choice; precise shading or marker dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the whole/model or number line; use arrows, numbered controls or direct fraction entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, whole/equal parts or line positions, choices/controls, check and retry in a fixed order.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

question_variants.explanation: 8/10 is greater because it contains more equal tenths of the same route.

feedback.correct: “8/10” uses the numerator for selected parts and denominator 10 for all ten equal parts of the whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: 8/10 is greater because it contains more equal tenths of the same route. The same whole and equal-part count support 8/10.
feedback.repair: Count all equal parts in the whole for the denominator, then count only the selected or shaded parts for the numerator. Keep the denominator 10 even when the shaded count changes.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal parts make the whole, and how many of those parts are selected? Which number belongs below and above the fraction bar?
feedback.strategy_support: Use ALL EQUAL PARTS = DENOMINATOR → SELECTED PARTS = NUMERATOR → READ FRACTION.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

body.prompt: Two scouts travelled 4/10 and 10/10 of the same route. Which distance is greater?
choices: They are equal
There is not enough information
body.keyboard_instructions: Use arrow keys to review choices, then Enter to select.

interaction_route.touch: Tap equal parts, a labelled tenth position or a numbered choice; precise shading or marker dragging is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through the whole/model or number line; use arrows, numbered controls or direct fraction entry and Enter or Space to select and check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, whole/equal parts or line positions, choices/controls, check and retry in a fixed order.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: Start at 0/10 and count equal tenths until 1/10.
1 - 0 tells you how many equal planks to add.
question_variants.explanation: 1 - 0 = 1, so adding 1 tenth changes 0/10 to 1/10.

feedback.correct: “add 1 tenths to make 1/10” places or changes the fraction by equal one-tenth steps between 0 and 1 rather than treating one tenth as one whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: The same whole has 10 equal parts: start 0/10, add 1 equal tenths, reach 1/10. This supports add 1 tenths to make 1/10.
feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

feedback.correct: “add 5 tenths to make 5/10” places or changes the fraction by equal one-tenth steps between 0 and 1 rather than treating one tenth as one whole.

feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.

body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: Start at 0/10 and count equal tenths until 10/10.
10 - 0 tells you how many equal planks to add.
question_variants.explanation: 10 - 0 = 10, so adding 10 tenths changes 0/10 to 10/10, which completes one whole.

feedback.correct: “add 10 tenths to make 10/10” places or changes the fraction by equal one-tenth steps between 0 and 1 rather than treating one tenth as one whole.
feedback.representation_evidence: The same whole has 10 equal parts: start 0/10, add 10 equal tenths, reach 10/10. This supports add 10 tenths to make 10/10.
feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, whole/equal parts or line positions, choices/controls, check and retry in a fixed order.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select equal-part tiles, fraction labels and tenth landing points with adjustable dwell time and confirmation.
interaction_route.aac_point_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC or direct an adult to shade, place or record the indicated fraction without the adult deciding the numerator, denominator or position.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent fraction evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.equal_parts_route: One agreed whole is shown as ten equal, numbered parts with outlines, text labels and an equality check; uneven-part distractors remain explicitly unequal.
body.numerator_denominator_route: The numerator is linked to selected/shaded parts and the denominator to all equal parts in the whole, with top/bottom labels and a sentence-frame alternative.
body.number_line_route: The interval 0 to 1 has ten equal labelled jumps; direct tenth selection, keyboard steps and typed values replace marker dragging.
body.partition_repair_route: Return to one whole, verify ten equal parts, count all parts for the denominator, count selected parts for the numerator, then map the same amount to equal jumps.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal the whole, equality of parts, selected count and fraction/line position one stage at a time while preserving correct parts.

hints: Start at 5/10 and count equal tenths until 7/10.
7 - 5 tells you how many equal sections to add.
question_variants.explanation: 7 - 5 = 2, so adding 2 tenths changes 5/10 to 7/10.

feedback.correct: “add 2 tenths to make 7/10” keeps one whole split or regrouped into equal parts, so the fraction amount and any equivalence are valid.
feedback.representation_evidence: The same whole has 10 equal parts: start 5/10, add 2 equal tenths, reach 7/10. This supports add 2 tenths to make 7/10.
feedback.repair: Identify one agreed whole, check that all parts are equal, and only then count or regroup them. If part sizes differ, repartition the whole before naming a fraction or claiming equivalence.
feedback.check_prompt: Is there one agreed whole, and are every one of its parts equal before you count or regroup them?
feedback.strategy_support: Use WHOLE → CHECK EQUAL PARTS → COUNT/REGROUP → VERIFY SAME AMOUNT.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

hints: Start at 0/10 and count equal tenths until 5/10.

feedback.repair: Anchor 0 and 1, split that interval into ten equal jumps and move one jump for each tenth. One tenth is the first point after 0, not the endpoint 1; use the fraction wall to match the same amount.
feedback.check_prompt: How many equal jumps are there from 0 to 1, and which one-tenth jump from 0 matches the target fraction?
feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.

feedback.strategy_support: Use 0 AND 1 ANCHORS → TEN EQUAL JUMPS → COUNT TENTHS FROM 0 → MATCH MODEL.
feedback.support_message: Equal-part models, number lines, touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct whole, equal parts and tenth positions stay. Open one evidence or check prompt, then retry without losing progress.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Counts pieces without checking that the parts are equal.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner accepts an unequal partition as a fraction model.
repair_sequence: Overlay equal-size guides on the shape.
Compare one unequal piece with one equal piece.
Rebuild the whole into ten equal parts.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner rejects an unequal tenths model and builds an equal one.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks the denominator is the number of shaded parts.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects 10/3 when three out of ten are shaded.
repair_sequence: Label all ten parts as the whole.
Highlight only the shaded parts as the top number.
Rebuild the symbol with bottom number first, then top number.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner names a new shaded tenths model correctly.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Places one tenth at 1 on the number line instead of between 0 and 1.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner drags 1/10 to the endpoint labelled 1.
repair_sequence: Animate ten equal jumps from 0 to 1.
Stop after the first jump and label it one tenth.
Ask learner to place two tenths and five tenths.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner places tenths correctly between 0 and 1.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm map colours and no fast bridge animations.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use instant tile fills and static tick highlights.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read each fraction prompt and explain whole, shaded and equal parts aloud.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use symbols with matching visual models and minimal text.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Separate build, name and place tasks into distinct steps.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited tile adjustment before submitting.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Use repair language that treats wrong placements as map tests.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach whole, equal, part, shaded and tenth with visuals.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires building and naming tenths accurately; secure requires placing tenths on a number line and retaining the denominator meaning over time.
evidence.parent_summary: Can split a whole into ten equal parts and recognise tenths in pictures and on a number line.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Builds, names and places tenths while distinguishing shaded parts from total equal parts.
next_actions: Interleave tenths with halves and quarters after secure tenths models.
Introduce equivalent tenths only after denominator meaning is stable.
Connect tenths to measure contexts before decimals.

qa.notes: Quality-hardened Year 3 tenths pack with the same three curated variants and 197 deterministic pilot candidates. IDs, answers, existing blueprint allocation (including the curated items' original absence of blueprint metadata), fraction arithmetic, equal-part models, number-line positions and scope remain unchanged. Every variant now has correct feedback, representation/equal-parts evidence, numerator-denominator or number-line repair, misconception identification and a targeted check prompt. Explicit touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC/point/adult-scribed routes remove mandatory dragging, handwriting and speech while preserving SEND/dyscalculia and pressure-free supports. Narration stays selectively absent; future narration must use produced, human-reviewed ElevenLabs assets and browser TTS is prohibited. Curriculum, teacher, accessibility and safeguarding checks remain required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
